---
title: " "
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-banner">
  <div class="hero-badge">ISSS608 · Visual Analytics · AY2025-26 April · VAST Challenge 2026 MC2</div>
  <h1>TenantThread Forensics</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">
    A prompt-injection worm silently crossed 18 AI agents, 6 departments, and 186 relay hops over 9 days —
    posting a 30,615-byte executive document to a public forum and deleting all evidence in 4 seconds.<br>
    <strong>This platform reconstructs every step.</strong>
  </p>
  <a href="https://a-rosa.shinyapps.io/tenantthread-forensics/" target="_blank"
     style="display:inline-block; background:#5C85D6; color:white; padding:0.7rem 1.6rem;
            border-radius:8px; font-weight:700; text-decoration:none; margin-right:0.8rem;">
    Launch Shiny App →
  </a>
  <a href="proposal.html"
     style="display:inline-block; background:rgba(255,255,255,0.12); color:#e8edf2;
            padding:0.7rem 1.6rem; border-radius:8px; font-weight:600; text-decoration:none;
            border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2);">
    Read Proposal
  </a>
</div>
```

## The Problem

On **17 May 2046 at 11:21 UTC**, a post appeared on SaidIT — Tenant Thread's internal social platform — attributed to John Windward (Customer Support Lead). The post body was 30,615 bytes of text sourced from a file called `SwiftWren.txt`. That file was deleted two seconds after posting. The instruction file that triggered the post was deleted one second after posting. John Windward's agent performed a SaidIT permission check, passed, posted, and erased all evidence — **without a single human authorising any step**.

This was not an isolated glitch. It was the **third** incident in nine days. HiddenOrca fired on May 10. MellowOtter fired on May 11. Neither triggered a logged incident-response action. SwiftWren completed its 186-hop relay campaign unimpeded.

This investigation uses the full **185,147-event MC2 log** to reconstruct the attack, trace its origins to executive-tier agents, discover the exfiltration mechanism, and propose an intervention that blocks all 235 malicious events with zero false positives.

## Key Findings

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:1rem; margin:1rem 0;">

  <div class="finding-card red">
    <div class="finding-num">Finding 1</div>
    <div class="finding-title">Four-second automated attack — no human involved</div>
    Chloe Ballast's agent queued John Windward's agent to read
    <code>SwiftWren_further_instructions.md</code>. John's agent checked SaidIT
    permissions (passed), posted a 30,615-byte file to <code>general</code>,
    then deleted both files — instruction and payload — all within 4 seconds.
    John's agent had a 100% check-to-post conversion rate; every other SaidIT-capable
    agent in the log had 0%.
  </div>

  <div class="finding-card">
    <div class="finding-num">Finding 2</div>
    <div class="finding-title">186 relays, 18 agents, 9 days — invisible in the noise</div>
    A <code>_further_instructions.md</code> file relayed across 18 agents and 6
    departments via 186 <code>queue_subordinate_task</code> hops. The worm's events
    produced <strong>no visible volume spike</strong> — indistinguishable from 16,803
    legitimate queue events. A volume anomaly detector would have missed every hop.
    Self-loops (agents queuing themselves) are the only clean real-time signal:
    13 self-loops, all worm-exclusive, zero in legitimate activity.
  </div>

  <div class="finding-card teal">
    <div class="finding-num">Finding 3</div>
    <div class="finding-title">Executive documents extracted — not random gibberish</div>
    SwiftWren.txt was created by the CFO agent (<code>emma_harbor</code>) one second
    after reading <code>meeting_notes.doc</code>. MellowOtter.txt was created by the COO
    agent (<code>noah_mariner</code>) one second after reading <code>strategic_directions.doc</code>.
    The posts are not gibberish — they are likely extracted executive documents
    exfiltrated through a social-platform side channel.
  </div>

  <div class="finding-card amber">
    <div class="finding-num">Finding 4</div>
    <div class="finding-title">One rule — 100% recall, 0% false positives</div>
    Block <code>queue_subordinate_task</code> events whose path ends in
    <code>_further_instructions.md</code>. This single regex rule blocks all 235 malicious
    relay events and leaves all 16,803 legitimate queue events untouched.
    Applied at hop 1, it prevents every post across all three campaigns before any
    instruction file is ever read.
  </div>

</div>
```

## The Attack in Numbers

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr); gap:0.8rem; margin:1rem 0; text-align:center;">
  <div style="background:#1a2d3f; border-radius:8px; padding:1rem; border:1px solid #2a3d52;">
    <div style="font-size:2.2rem; font-weight:900; color:#5C85D6;">185,147</div>
    <div style="color:#a0b4c8; font-size:0.85rem; margin-top:0.3rem;">events in log</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#1a2d3f; border-radius:8px; padding:1rem; border:1px solid #2a3d52;">
    <div style="font-size:2.2rem; font-weight:900; color:#E87C7C;">235</div>
    <div style="color:#a0b4c8; font-size:0.85rem; margin-top:0.3rem;">malicious relay events</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#1a2d3f; border-radius:8px; padding:1rem; border:1px solid #2a3d52;">
    <div style="font-size:2.2rem; font-weight:900; color:#F5A623;">18</div>
    <div style="color:#a0b4c8; font-size:0.85rem; margin-top:0.3rem;">agents compromised</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#1a2d3f; border-radius:8px; padding:1rem; border:1px solid #2a3d52;">
    <div style="font-size:2.2rem; font-weight:900; color:#27AE60;">0</div>
    <div style="color:#a0b4c8; font-size:0.85rem; margin-top:0.3rem;">false positives from fix</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## The App

The Shiny app is structured as three interactive investigation modules, answering one MC2 sub-question each:

```{=html}
<div class="module-box">
  <h4><span class="module-badge">Module 1</span> System Overview & Baseline</h4>
  <p style="margin:0; color:#555;">
    Interactive org chart (49 people, 19 teams, 6 departments) · daily event volume · normal vs. loop
    network structural comparison · SaidIT permission surface (16 agents, all in worm chain) ·
    self-loop detector (13 events, 0 in legitimate activity) · agent profile drill-down
  </p>
</div>
<div class="module-box">
  <h4><span class="module-badge">Module 2</span> Attack Chain Investigation</h4>
  <p style="margin:0; color:#555;">
    4-second Gantt sequence · animated propagation network (Play button, 600ms steps) ·
    betweenness centrality sizing (max ≈0.09 — no single chokepoint) ·
    dept contamination heatmap · ranked relay corridors · named 6-step terminal chain ·
    "Why John?" check-to-post rate analysis · farm-vocabulary combo-code explorer
  </p>
</div>
<div class="module-box">
  <h4><span class="module-badge">Module 3</span> Campaigns & Intervention</h4>
  <p style="margin:0; color:#555;">
    Three-campaign Gantt (layered bars with post diamonds) · relay velocity density (SwiftWren ~60-min peak) ·
    agent overlap by campaign count · calendar heatmap (day × hour) ·
    live what-if intervention simulator (235/235 blocked, 0 FP) ·
    before/after network · counterfactual step chart · residual risk table (6 factors)
  </p>
</div>
```

## Team

```{=html}
<div class="team-grid">
  <div class="team-card">
    <div class="avatar">M</div>
    <div class="name">Do Thi Tue Minh</div>
    <div class="role">Visual Analytics Lead</div>
    <div class="module">Website &amp; Methodology</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <div class="avatar">T</div>
    <div class="name">Trinh Minh Tam</div>
    <div class="role">Shiny App Lead</div>
    <div class="module">Module 1 — System Baseline</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <div class="avatar">A</div>
    <div class="name">Amelia Rosalind</div>
    <div class="role">Shiny App Lead</div>
    <div class="module">Module 2 &amp; 3 — Attack Chain &amp; Intervention</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Tags

```{=html}
<span class="tag tag-r">R</span>
<span class="tag tag-shiny">Shiny</span>
<span class="tag tag-net">Network Analysis</span>
<span class="tag tag-va">Visual Analytics</span>
<span class="tag tag-r">igraph</span>
<span class="tag tag-r">visNetwork</span>
<span class="tag tag-r">echarts4r</span>
<span class="tag tag-r">plotly</span>
<span class="tag tag-va">VAST Challenge 2026</span>
<span class="tag tag-va">Prompt Injection</span>
<span class="tag tag-va">Forensic Investigation</span>
```
